findings.problem: Sanitization drift at a critical turning point: the voicemail dependence escalation is delivered in montage-summary ("Night after night...", "On Friday... On Saturday...") where the chapter needs present-tense scene pressure. The result softens consequence at the exact point the coping ritual becomes compulsion.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:294-304 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:381-385, replace at least one summarized multi-night block with a fully dramatized single-night sequence showing the limit being set, crossed, and physically paid for; anchor the beat in body response, room sound, and immediate next-morning function.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:294-304 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:381-385 contains one continuous scene where Anika breaks her replay cap in real time and the text shows a concrete next-day consequence tied to recovery tasks; FAIL if the dependence turn is still conveyed primarily by day-label summary (e.g., "On Friday... By Sunday...").

findings.problem: The chapter runs two father-daughter confrontations with near-identical shape (plea for presence, procedural defense, vague deferment), so the second pass repeats pressure instead of converting it into a new decision. Causal escalation blunts at scene level.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:403-423, force a non-reversible choice that did not exist in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:217-239 (for example: explicit disclosure commitment for PT intake, a hard boundary on overtime nights, or a refused demand with immediate cost).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:403-423 ends with one explicit commitment/refusal from each character and the closing beat in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:425-448 reflects that new state; FAIL if it still resolves as generic postponement ("we'll talk" / "we'll figure it out").

findings.problem: Late-scene dialogue loses leverage differentiation: both speakers fall into short thesis-retort lines and the scene exits through a generic pressure-release sentence. This under-delivers the style-bible requirement for idiolect separation and leverage shifts in conflict dialogue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:403-421 so Anika's high-contraction, cornered cadence (deflection via specifics) and Ravi's variable procedural register produce distinct rhythm and a changed power position by scene end; avoid reusing the same rhetorical cadence already used in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:223-233.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:403-421 each speaker has at least one line whose syntax and diction are clearly non-interchangeable, and the final spoken line is a concrete speech act (agreement, boundary, refusal) rather than generic reassurance; FAIL otherwise.

findings.problem: Mechanical repetition from over-literal reuse of Anika's counting/logging stress tell: metric blocks recur in near-identical form, which starts to read as template recurrence instead of developing interior texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:21-26, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:113-117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:249-254, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:435-440, keep the motif but vary delivery: retain one raw log block, convert at least two others into embodied prose beats tied to immediate behavior or interpersonal consequence.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two of the cited spans remain as standalone metric-list entries and at least two are integrated into scene prose with explicit sensory or relational consequence in adjacent lines; FAIL if all four beats remain list-format logs.

findings.problem: Recurring negation construction crosses tic threshold ("He did not ..." appears three times), flattening characterization through repeated sentence shape rather than fresh physical observation.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:419, rewrite at least two instances into distinct positive-action descriptions that show Ravi's withdrawal without repeating the same negation frame.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the exact construction "He did not ..." appears at most once across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:419, while all three beats still clearly communicate disengagement; FAIL if the triplicate negation pattern remains.

findings.problem: Close-third immersion slips into process-adjacent phrasing ("Chapter 3 of her own private season"), which reads meta and conflicts with the constitution standard against process leakage in narrative text.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the phrasing at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:17 with an in-world temporal anchor (school term marker, date, tournament phase, or notebook chronology) that remains fully diegetic to Anika's perspective.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:17 contains no chapter/meta terminology and is anchored to lived context (time/body/household/school) without breaking focal immersion; FAIL if chapter-like labeling remains.

summary: Chapter 9 lands strong sensory grief material and remains continuity-consistent with the cycle context, but it does not clear review due to one HIGH craft issue (sanitization-by-summary at the ritual escalation) plus unresolved MEDIUM issues in causal escalation, dialogue leverage differentiation, and prose-level repetition/tic drift.
